name: a-distant-mirror
description: Barbara Tuchman's A Distant Mirror — medieval history toolkit examining the calamitous 14th century through plague, war, church schism, and social upheaval, mirroring our own troubled times
useCase: Understand the Black Death, Hundred Years War, church crisis, medieval society, and the parallels between the 14th century and modern era
knownRisks: Tuchman's interpretive framework (the 'distant mirror' thesis) is a narrative device, not a rigorous historical argument
Some specialists have criticized her use of a single noble (de Coucy) as representative of the entire century
The book was published in 1978 — scholarship on the 14th century has evolved since then
